title: ds-viz-markdown - option matrix (dark)
description: Test bench for splunk.markdown. Verifies all options from the 10.4 PDF: backgroundColor, fontColor, fontSize (extraSmall|small|default|large|extraLarge), markdown content. Demonstrates basic text formatting, headings, lists, tables, code blocks, blockquotes, links, images, fontFamily, color combinations, and editorial layout patterns.

options.markdown:
# `splunk.markdown` - option matrix

Markdown panels are the **typographic backbone** of every dashboard - section headers, panel descriptions, KPI explanations, editorial framing, runbook links.

Full options list from the **10.4 PDF**:
- `backgroundColor`
- `fontColor`
- `fontSize` (`extraSmall` | `small` | `default` | `large` | `extraLarge`)
- `markdown` (string content)

Also supported by the renderer (schema-validated):
- `fontFamily` - one of seven named fonts: `Splunk Platform Sans`, `Splunk Data Sans`, `Splunk Platform Mono`, `Arial`, `Helvetica`, `Times New Roman`, `Comic Sans MS` (or a DOS token). CSS stacks like `Georgia, serif` are **rejected**.

**Important**: `fontSize`, `fontColor`, and `fontFamily` apply to the **entire panel uniformly**. There is no way to mix sizes/fonts/colors inside a single markdown body - use multiple panels for that.

Markdown supports **all standard Markdown syntax** except raw HTML. See the panels below for verified patterns.

viz_default.title: 1. Default markdown
viz_default.description: No options set - inherits theme defaults.

options.markdown:
This is the **default** markdown panel.

Nothing set: `fontColor` falls back to the theme default, `backgroundColor` is transparent, and `fontSize` is `default` (~14px for unformatted text).

Use this as the *baseline* for editorial copy.

## Heading on extraSmall

Tiny print - footnotes and disclaimers.

Note: `fontSize` scales the **entire panel uniformly**. To get five sizes side-by-side you need five separate panels (this panel and the four below).

## Heading on small

Metadata, source attribution, secondary copy.

## Heading on default

Everything else - the workhorse size.

## Heading on large

Callouts that should compete with panel headers.

## Heading on extraLarge

Hero copy and dashboard subtitles.

## Status: All systems normal

This entire panel renders in `#26A69A` (brand teal) via `fontColor`.

Use this for **single-message status panels** - the color *is* the signal.

## Critical zone

Darker `backgroundColor` makes this panel visually distinct from the dashboard grid.

Useful for *grouping* related panels - put a colored markdown above them as a section title.

## INCIDENT

Services in `prod-eu-west` are experiencing elevated 5xx rates.

**On-call**: Alex Tan  
**Runbook**: `runbooks/eu-west-incident.md`

*Use red-on-dark sparingly - reserve it for actual incidents.*

### Unordered list

- North America
- EMEA
- APAC

### Ordered list

1. Receive alert
2. Acknowledge in PagerDuty
3. Open the runbook
4. Follow the diagnostic flow
5. Resolve or escalate

### Nested

- Production
  - `prod-us-east`
  - `prod-eu-west`
  - `prod-apac`
- Staging
  - `stage-us-east`
- Development

### KPI quick reference

| Metric | Threshold | Action |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Error rate | > 1% | Page on-call |
| p95 latency | > 800 ms | Investigate |
| CPU | > 85% | Scale out |
| Disk | > 90% | Page SRE |
| Saturation | > 80% | Pre-warn capacity team |

*Note: tables can't be sorted or paginated - use `splunk.table` if interaction is needed.*

Use `index=main sourcetype=access_combined` to filter web access logs.

```
index=main sourcetype=access_combined status>=500
| stats count BY host
| sort - count
| head 10
```

The code block has syntax-friendly grey background that extends to the panel edge.

Inline `code` keeps the grey background tight to the token.

> **Why this matters**: every minute of downtime in `checkout` costs ~$8k. The on-call playbook below is *non-negotiable*.

1. **Acknowledge** within 5 min.
2. **Triage** using the runbook diagnostic tree.
3. **Communicate** to the incident channel.
4. **Resolve or escalate** within 15 min.

Return to *normal operations* once the green checkmark appears in the SLO panel.

### Quick links

- [Runbook](https://example.com/runbook) - on-call diagnostic flow
- [Dashboard catalog](https://example.com/dashboards) - all team dashboards
- [Status page](https://status.example.com) - public-facing
- [Incident channel](https://chat.example.com/incidents) - real-time coordination

Links open in a new tab when the dashboard is rendered. Use [reference-style](https://example.com) links for repeated URLs.

[ref]: https://example.com

[Same destination][ref] using the reference.

## Editorial story-frame

*"At 09:42 UTC, latency in the EU region began climbing past the 800 ms warning threshold..."*

This panel uses Times New Roman, the only serif in the allowed list. Reads as *editorial* against the platform's sans default.

## Runbook excerpt

```
2025-04-26T09:42:18Z latency=842ms region=eu-west
2025-04-26T09:42:21Z latency=901ms region=eu-west
2025-04-26T09:42:24Z latency=1024ms region=eu-west
```

Mono-spaced font for log excerpts and code-leaning copy.

## Splunk Data Sans

The number-friendly variant of the Splunk type system. Use for KPI annotations and tabular numeric callouts.
